result:
Trace complete, confirmed directly in source. Summary:

## Call chain (x86_64 `read()`)

1. **`SYSCALL_DEFINE3(read, ...)`** — `fs/read_write.c:621`
   Expands via `include/linux/syscalls.h:221,228,240` (`SYSCALL_DEFINEx`/`__SYSCALL_DEFINEx`) into the actual symbol `sys_read()`.
2. → **`ksys_read(fd, buf, count)`** — called at `fs/read_write.c:623`, defined at `fs/read_write.c:602`
   - resolves `fd` via `fdget_pos()`, computes `ppos`
3. → **`vfs_read(f.file, buf, count, ppos)`** — called at `fs/read_write.c:613`, defined at `fs/read_write.c:450`
   - checks `FMODE_READ`/`FMODE_CAN_READ`, `access_ok()`, calls `rw_verify_area()`, then dispatches to `file->f_op->read()` or `new_sync_read()` (via `->read_iter`).

**Full hop list, file:line:**
- `fs/read_write.c:621` — `SYSCALL_DEFINE3(read, unsigned int, fd, char __user *, buf, size_t, count)`
- `fs/read_write.c:602` — `ksys_read()`
- `fs/read_write.c:450` — `vfs_read()`

## Syscall-number → handler mapping

In-tree source table: **`arch/x86/entry/syscalls/syscall_64.tbl:11`**
```
0	common	read			sys_read
```
This `.tbl` file is the hand-maintained in-tree source (not itself generated); build scripts (`scripts/syscall.tbl`-processing under `arch/x86/entry/syscalls/Makefile`) consume it to generate the actual `sys_call_table`/headers (e.g. `arch/x86/include/generated/asm/syscalls_64.h`, used by `arch/x86/entry/syscall_64.c`). So syscall #0 on x86_64 maps to `sys_read`, which is exactly the symbol `SYSCALL_DEFINE3(read, ...)` expands to at `fs/read_write.c:621`.
